Package org.solenopsis.soap.tooling

Class OmniSupervisorConfig

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.OmniSupervisorConfig
public class OmniSupervisorConfig extends Metadata

Java class for OmniSupervisorConfig complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="OmniSupervisorConfig">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}Metadata">
       <sequence>
         <element name="isTimelineHidden" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean"/>
         <element name="masterLabel" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="omniSupervisorConfigAction" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}OmniSupervisorConfigAction" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="omniSupervisorConfigGroup" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}OmniSupervisorConfigGroup" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="omniSupervisorConfigProfile" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}OmniSupervisorConfigProfile" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="omniSupervisorConfigQueue" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}OmniSupervisorConfigQueue" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="omniSupervisorConfigSkill" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}OmniSupervisorConfigSkill" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="skillVisibility" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}OmniSuperSkillVisibilityType" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

    • getOmniSupervisorConfigAction

      public List<OmniSupervisorConfigAction> getOmniSupervisorConfigAction()
      Gets the value of the omniSupervisorConfigAction property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the omniSupervisorConfigAction property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getOmniSupervisorConfigAction().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list OmniSupervisorConfigAction

      Returns:
      The value of the omniSupervisorConfigAction property.

    • getOmniSupervisorConfigGroup

      Gets the value of the omniSupervisorConfigGroup property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getOmniSupervisorConfigGroup().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list OmniSupervisorConfigGroup

      Returns:
      The value of the omniSupervisorConfigGroup property.
